extraction.one_line_summary: VAN POPPEL Wout won from the breakaway while POGAČAR repeatedly attacked VINGEGAARD in final kilometer but gap remained frozen at 10 seconds.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR's poker-style feint attacks in sub-1km on steep finishes cannot break VINGEGAARD when yellow jersey wheel-locks perfectly.
UAE sandwiched VINGEGAARD with leadout teammates on final climb hoping to disrupt positioning before POGAČAR's acceleration.
NEILANDS Krists attacked prematurely from 23-rider break on non-categorized terrain with excessive distance remaining, tactical error.
CRADDOCK Lawson finished fourth, suggesting Jayco planted multiple breakaway options early when composition seemed permissive.

rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Wout: positive: first Tour stage win in 10th participation suggests breakaway reading skills matured late-career.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: three consecutive acceleration attempts in final 800m showed power but predictability against locked-on VINGEGAARD.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: defended 10-second gap through multiple late accelerations without counterattacking, conserving energy for upcoming TT.
rider_signals.NEILANDS Krists: negative: burned matches attacking from 23-man break far from categorized climbs, poor breakaway economy.
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: fourth place finish reveals Jayco's aggressive break infiltration strategy paying dividends.
rider_signals.PETIT Adrien: negative: still suffering from prior day's crash injuries, vulnerability persists on multi-stage Alpine blocks.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: crashed mid-stage in large pileage, continued crash susceptibility pattern through this Tour.
